response:
text  
paragraph 1  
the condition you are referring to is called ectopic pregnancy, where the fertilized egg implants in the fallopian tube instead of the uterus. this can lead to the formation of a mass that may not be felt during a routine check-up. the symptoms include abdominal pain, vaginal bleeding, and sometimes a missed period. it is important to seek medical attention immediately if you experience these symptoms.  
paragraph 2  
the condition can be life-threatening if not treated promptly. it may require surgery or medication to remove the affected tissue. in some cases, the pregnancy may be lost naturally. the key is to monitor the situation closely and follow the guidance of a healthcare provider.
